_comment: Smart Engine question bank. Tap-to-ask only (no free-text NLP — see PIB Smart Engine notes). Each entry maps a question chip to a computeKey read from existing APP analytics (no new computation). label = chip text (i18n key, resolved via srT()). scope = which mode(s) the question applies to. answerTemplate uses {placeholders} filled by js/smart-engine.js from APP data at answer time — this file has NO logic, only data, so it can be translated/edited without touching JS.
deflectionMessage: That's outside what I can help with — I only answer questions about this class's uploaded data. Try one of the questions below.

questions.label: Which subject is weakest in this class?
questions.answerTemplate: {topSubject} needs attention. {topPctBelow}% of your class is below the pass threshold, and the class average ({topAvg}%) is the lowest of all subjects. {spreadNote}
questions.spreadNoteWide: This isn't a few weak students — it's spread across the class, which usually points to teaching pace or material difficulty, not individual gaps. Consider extra classes or a teaching-method review for {topSubject}.
questions.spreadNoteNarrow: A smaller group is driving this average down, so targeted support for those students may help more than a class-wide change.

questions.label: Which subject is strongest in this class?
questions.answerTemplate: {bottomSubject} is this class's strongest subject — class average {bottomAvg}%, with only {bottomPctBelow}% of students below the pass threshold. Worth understanding what's working here; it may be replicable in weaker subjects.

questions.label: How is the class performing overall?
questions.answerTemplate: Of {n} students: {excellent} are excelling (80%+), {good} doing well (60-79%), {average} average (35-59%), and {below} below pass threshold. {distributionNote}

questions.label: Does attendance affect performance here?
questions.answerTemplate: Students with zero absences average {noAbsenceAvg}% ({noAbsenceN} students); those with any absence average {someAbsenceAvg}% ({someAbsenceN} students) — a {gapPoints}-point gap. {attendanceNote}
questions.unavailableMessage: Not enough students in one of the attendance groups yet to report a meaningful comparison.

questions.label: Is there a gender performance gap?
questions.answerTemplate: {leadGroup} lead by {overallGap} points overall. The biggest subject gap is in {maxGapSubject} ({maxGapValue} points), where {maxGapLead} are ahead.
questions.noGapMessage: No meaningful overall gap between groups in this class.
questions.unavailableMessage: Gender comparison isn't available — either no Gender column was found, or there aren't enough students with a recognised value in both groups.

questions.label: Which students are statistical outliers?
questions.answerTemplate: {count} student(s) are statistical outliers vs the class: {list}. These are worth a closer look either way — unusually far ahead may mean enrichment opportunity, unusually far behind may need urgent support.
questions.emptyMessage: No statistical outliers detected — the class average is fairly evenly spread this term.

questions.label: Are there natural student groups I should know about?
questions.answerTemplate: This class naturally groups into {k} clusters: {groupList}. Each group may benefit from a different approach — e.g. the "Needs Support" group may need remedial classes, while "High but Volatile" may need consistency coaching rather than more content.
questions.unavailableMessage: This analysis needs at least 30 students for meaningful groups to emerge — not available for this class size.

questions.label: Who moved up or down in rank recently?
questions.answerTemplate: Since the last test: {upCount} students moved up in class rank, {downCount} moved down. Notable movers: {notableList}. Rank movement often shows a change before it appears in the overall average — worth an early check-in with those declining.
questions.unavailableMessage: Rank movement needs at least 2 tests recorded to compare — not available yet.

questions.label: Why is this student ranked where they are?
questions.answerTemplate: {name} is ranked {rank} of {n}, {topperGap} points behind the class topper. {gapNote}

questions.label: Is this student improving or declining?
What might they score next test?
questions.answerTemplate: Based on their trend across recorded tests, {name} is projected to score around {predictedNext}% next test. {predictionNote}
questions.unavailableMessage: Needs at least 2 tests recorded to project a next score.

questions.label: How consistent is this student across tests?
Is this student at wellbeing risk?
What's this student's overall health score?
questions.answerTemplate: {name}'s overall health score is {healthScore}/100 — {healthBand}. This blends academic performance, consistency, trend, and engagement.

questions.label: How does this student compare to the class in each subject?
Is their class rank moving up or down right now?
questions.answerTemplate: {name}'s class rank has {direction} by {absMovement} position(s) since the last test.
questions.unavailableMessage: Needs at least 2 tests recorded to compare rank movement.
